ID:       ddx-937de9fc
Title:    skills/personas/plugins: install-to-agent vs install-in-project — and ddx plugin commands honor both
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   area:config, area:plugin, area:cli, kind:feature, phase:3, initiative:zero-footprint, principle:skills-personas
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-602ae31a
Deps:     ddx-06cbaa90, ddx-04566a8f, ddx-06f820f4, ddx-64ecd687, ddx-c10d5af2
Desc:     PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

../docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md
---
ddx:
  id: ADR-027
  depends_on:
    - FEAT-011
    - FEAT-015
    - ADR-003
---
# ADR-027: Skill Install Topology and Resolution Precedence

**Status:** Accepted
**Date:** 2026-06-01

## Context

DDx ships the `ddx` skill as a package, and operators need to know which
install layer they are actually using. Before this decision, the install
topology was spread across implementation details and a handful of docs:

- the project-local package install under `<project>/.ddx/plugins/ddx/`;
- the global fallback under `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`;
- the baked-in default package embedded in the binary;
- the agent-facing skill outputs under `.agents/skills/` and
  `.claude/skills/`.

Without one documented precedence rule, `ddx doctor` could not tell whether a
project was using its own package copy or falling through to the global layer,
and the agent docs had no single place to describe where the skill surfaces
live.

data.output: 207:## Persona System

1	---
2	ddx:
3	  id: ADR-027
4	  depends_on:
5	    - FEAT-011
6	    - FEAT-015
7	    - ADR-003
8	---
9	# ADR-027: Skill Install Topology and Resolution Precedence
10	
11	**Status:** Accepted
12	**Date:** 2026-06-01
13	
14	## Context
15	
16	DDx ships the `ddx` skill as a package, and operators need to know which
17	install layer they are actually using. Before this decision, the install
18	topology was spread across implementation details and a handful of docs:
19	
20	- the project-local package install under `<project>/.ddx/plugins/ddx/`;
21	- the global fallback under `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`;
22	- the baked-in default package embedded in the binary;
23	- the agent-facing skill outputs under `.agents/skills/` and
24	  `.claude/skills/`.
25	
26	Without one documented precedence rule, `ddx doctor` could not tell whether a
27	project was using its own package copy or falling through to the global layer,
28	and the agent docs had no single place to describe where the skill surfaces
29	live.
30	
31	## Decision
32	
33	DDx resolves the default `ddx` package in this order:
34	
35	1. project-local `<project>/.ddx/plugins/ddx/`
36	2. global `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`
37	3. baked-in package embedded in the binary
38	
39	The project-local package is the authoritative install for a repository. The
40	global layer is a fallback and may be used when the project copy is absent.
41	The baked-in layer exists only for the default `ddx` package so the binary
42	remains usable offline.
43	
44	The agent-facing skill outputs are project-local and live in:
45	
46	- `<project>/.agents/skills/ddx/`
47	- `<project>/.claude/skills/ddx/`
48	
49	Home-directory skill installs are retired. DDx does not add new state under
50	`~/.agents/skills` or `~/.claude/skills`.
51	
52	## Consequences
53	
54	- `ddx doctor` can report the project install and the global fallback as
55	  distinct states.
56	- Operators can distinguish `ok`, `missing`, and
57	  `lazy-resolves-to-global` for the project layer.
58	- The docs can point agents and operators to the same project-local surfaces
59	  instead of describing competing home-directory and project-directory
60	  locations.
61	- The default package remains available offline through the embedded layer.
62	
63

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"os"
6		"path/filepath"
7		"testing"
8	
9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/registry"
11		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
12		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
13	)
14	
15	// makeLocalPlugin creates a minimal plugin fixture at dir with the given name
16	// and one skill. It returns the skill directory path.
17	func makeLocalPlugin(t *testing.T, dir, name string) string {
18		t.Helper()
19		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "package.yaml"), []byte(
20			"name: "+name+"\n"+
21				"version: 1.0.0\n"+
22				"description: Test plugin\n"+
23				"type: plugin\n"+
24				"source: https://example.com/"+name+"\n"+
25				"api_version: \"1\"\n"+
26				"install:\n"+
27				"  root:\n"+
28				"    source: .\n"+
29				"    target: .ddx/plugins/"+name+"\n"+
30				"  skills:\n"+
31				"    - source: skills/\n"+
32				"      target: .agents/skills/\n"+
33				"    - source: skills/\n"+
34				"      target: .claude/skills/\n",
35		), 0o644))
36	
37		skillDir := filepath.Join(dir, "skills", name+"-skill")
38		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(skillDir, 0o755))
39		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(skillDir, "SKILL.md"), []byte(
40			"---\nname: "+name+"-skill\ndescription: Test skill\n---\n\nBody.\n",
41		), 0o644))
42		return skillDir
43	}
44	
45	// TestInstallGlobal_WritesXDGAndAgentTierLinks verifies AC1:
46	// ddx install <name> --global writes to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/
47	// and creates agent-tier links under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>.
48	func TestInstallGlobal_WritesXDGAndAgentTierLinks(t *testing.T) {
49		workDir := t.TempDir()
50		homeDir := t.TempDir()
51		xdgDataHome := t.TempDir()
52		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
53		t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDataHome)
54	
55		localPlugin := t.TempDir()
56		makeLocalPlugin(t, localPlugin, "myplugin")
57	
58		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
59		output, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "install", "myplugin", "--global", "--local", localPlugin, "--force")
60		require.NoError(t, err, output)
61	
62		// Plugin root must land in the global tree.
63		globalPluginDir := filepath.Join(xdgDataHome, "ddx", "global", "plugins", "myplugin")
64		info, statErr := os.Lstat(globalPluginDir)
65		require.NoError(t, statErr, "global plugin dir must exist")
66		assert.True(t, info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0, "global plugin dir must be a symlink to the local checkout")
67	
68		// Agent-tier skill links must be in the home directory.
69		// Local installs create symlinks, so use os.Lstat (DirExists follows symlinks).
70		agentSkill := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill")
71		claudeSkill := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "skills", "myplugin-skill")
72		agentInfo, agentErr := os.Lstat(agentSkill)
73		require.NoError(t, agentErr, "~/.agents/skills/<name> must exist for global install")
74		assert.True(t, agentInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || agentInfo.IsDir(),
75			"~/.agents/skills/<name> must be a symlink or dir for global install")
76		claudeInfo, claudeErr := os.Lstat(claudeSkill)
77		require.NoError(t, claudeErr, "~/.claude/skills/<name> must exist for global install")
78		assert.True(t, claudeInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || claudeInfo.IsDir(),
79			"~/.claude/skills/<name> must be a symlink or dir for global install")
80	
81		// Nothing must land in the project directory's skill dirs.
82		_, noAgentErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(workDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
83		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noAgentErr), "project .agents/skills must not be created for global install")
84		_, noClaudeErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(workDir, ".claude", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
85		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noClaudeErr), "project .claude/skills must not be created for global install")
86	}
87	
88	// TestInstallProject_InTreeLinks verifies AC2:
89	// ddx install <name> (no --global, in-tree mode) installs to <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/
90	// and creates project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and
91	// <project>/.agents/skills/<name>.
92	func TestInstallProject_InTreeLinks(t *testing.T) {
93		workDir := t.TempDir()
94		homeDir := t.TempDir()
95		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
96	
97		// Force in-tree mode by pre-creating .ddx/.
98		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(workDir, ddxroot.DirName), 0o755))
99	
100		localPlugin := t.TempDir()
